etymology.description: 人(人) (rén, "person") + 攴(攴) (pū, "strike" or "hit") -> depiction may include drops of water -> combines to suggest 'warning' or 'purification'
etymology.interpretation: The character 攸 is an ideogrammic compound with 人(人) (rén, "person") + 攴(攴) (pū, "strike" or "hit"). Two interpretations exist: one posits it as a depiction of a person about to be disciplined, thus suggesting 'warning'; another suggests a ritual washing, connected to ceremonial purification, and thus involves water symbolism. The sense of 'distant' likely emerged as a metaphorical extension from the idea of warning or cleansing, which sets one apart or corrects a path.
